response.justification: The candidate is a seasoned generalist with strong systems fundamentals — low-latency, event-driven microservices in DLang, async I/O, and multi-threaded backend work — but the role is explicitly a hands-on senior C++ position in industrial automation. His only professional C++ experience was a brief stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015, and his last decade has been dominated by TypeScript/Node.js web platforms. There is no evidence of MQTT, message brokers at the required depth, IEC 62443/SDL practice, or industrial protocol experience, making the core-competency gap too wide despite solid engineering craftsmanship.
strengths: Deep systems-programming experience with low-latency, high-throughput services (async I/O, fibers, caching) transferable to performance-critical C++ work
Strong software craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, testing tooling authorship, and mentoring/teaching track record
Broad full-stack and event-driven architecture experience, including Kubernetes-deployed geo-data platform
concerns: Essentially no recent professional C++/STL work — last C++ role ended in 2015
No industrial cybersecurity exposure (IEC 62443, SDL) or industrial protocols (MQTT, Modbus, OPC UA)
Career trajectory has moved toward web/product engineering, away from the embedded/industrial domain this role requires

key_factors.factor: Strong low-latency systems and architecture fundamentals
